pages.full_text: Charles & Eve paddling near sea lions on Great Blasket Island, In the distance is the Dingle peninsula.
photo_captions: Charles & Eve paddling near sea lions on Great Blasket Island, In the distance is the Dingle peninsula.
pages.notes: Page is dominated by a large photograph showing two figures on a sandy beach with an island in the background. Caption below the photo. No handwritten page number visible. 'sea lions' may be 'sea lions' or possibly 'seals' — transcribed as written.

pages.full_text:
near a pub, where we could have had supper. It is Tonic's (15 weeks) first camp. Pooted where he shouldn't. He slept in the boot box under the van. A fishing river, [illegible] reserved for B'ham. A lovely mile's walk  Sat 14.

On to the farm. Chatting in the sun. The boys swam 3 times. Simon & Jamie had a meeting. Kate & Eve & J&C picked blackberries & plums. Fruit perfect. Tea & on to Malvern. We took out TWP for W's birday dinner. Plus James (piano) Daniel (Peter's friend) & Tom, Ari & [?Russel] Tonic in a cage. Lovely, long (tiring) day.

__Wed Oct 23rd.__

Simon, William, Peter, John & Charles left at 10.35. for Chad to pack. Simon forgot his clothes & they left Westfield Rd. at 11.30. with them (Thomas & Eve had gone to visit Jane & Harry at Longwood, where Corning was, the night before.)

We all arrived at the Coda Centre, Munster Rd., Fulham, London at 2.30 - 3.15. to put out the food ready for the Chad showroom opening. It went very well with Charles passing the food, John playing
pages.notes: Page continues from the previous page (starts mid-sentence). No handwritten page number visible at top-right. 'Sat 14' date has no year; 'Wed Oct 23rd' year cannot be confirmed from this page alone — volume covers 1989-2003 but context (Tonic as 15-week-old puppy, Coda Centre/Chad showroom) does not pin the year. 'B'ham' is an abbreviation for Birmingham. 'TWP' is an abbreviation, possibly initials of a person. 'birday' appears to be a misspelling of 'birthday'. 'Russel' could also read 'Russell'. Text is cut off at the bottom — continues on next page.
